name: Federal Reserve
summary: **MAY CPI 4.2% (3-YR HIGH) LOCKS IN THE 'RATES-NOT-WAR' ENGINE.** The Fed's problem is no longer separable from the war: energy (war-derived via the Hormuz blockade) drove ~60% of the May monthly CPI increase to a 4.2% YoY headline, but core at 2.9% has its own momentum. Market pricing: a near-certain hold at the 17 Jun meeting, but a December HIKE is now priced as live base-risk (odds disputed 30->70%+ across CME FedWatch / Yahoo / Polymarket -- the graph's prior '~70%' read now looks like the top of the range). THE STRUCTURAL EDGE FOR THE INVESTOR: the crisis now transmits into US assets through the CPI/Fed channel (slow, sticky) rather than the war-premium channel (fast, reversible -- dead since late May). Even a signed Iran deal does NOT buy back this drawdown on a short horizon; the December-hike repricing is the regime fact under gold's collapse, the dollar's firmness, and the equity selloff.

active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS watching — but the market-implied odds of a December HIKE have ADVANCED to ~70% (from ~45% a week ago) on the hot May jobs print + oil-fed inflation. This is the closest the cycle has come, and the repricing is already the engine under the metals collapse, firm dollar (DXY ~99.9), and 4.52% 10Y. The trigger requires an actual FED signal/action; a market-implied repricing on a data print does NOT fire it. Document the ~70% odds; the 2Y at 3.63% (below the funds-range top) confirms a hike is priced/odds, not executed. Critical: this repricing would NOT fully reverse on Gulf de-escalation (sticky inflation + hot labour market are independent).

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: the December Fed-HIKE odds jumped to ~70% (from ~45% a week ago) on hot May jobs + oil-fed inflation, pinning the 10Y at 4.52% and lifting DXY to ~99.9 — the engine under the metals collapse and the non-bid of the war-risk complex. Weight stays 10 (formula caps; already at ceiling).

2026-06-06: the hot-jobs rate repricing compressed equity multiples — the rates co-driver (with the Broadcom AI crack) of the 5-June risk-off (S&P -2.6%, NASDAQ -4.2%). Weight stays 10 (formula caps; already at ceiling).

2026-06-06 (markets E1): ADDED. The hot May jobs print -> no cuts / hike priced -> higher 10Y compressed long-duration tech multiples directly into the Broadcom-led AI rout (SOX largest 1D plunge since March 2020). STRENGTHEN per the edge proposal; first explicit activation (act 1). Initial weight 5.0 for a 2nd-order systemic channel that genuinely fired (directness 1.5; not inflated on one session).

2026-06-10: the Fed-hike repricing (Dec hike ~70% from ~45%) lifted real yields + DXY (~99.9), repricing zero-yield gold DOWN 2.1% to a ~23-March low DURING a three-country missile strike on US installations — the channel by which the Iran shock is gold-NEGATIVE (shock->oil->inflation->yields/dollar->gold down). Weight stays 10 (formula caps; at ceiling).

recent_signals.headline: THE DEAL DOESN'T FIX THE FED. Oil fell 4% on de-escalation but the December-hike repricing held (10Y 4.49% flat) -- May CPI 4.2% does not un-print, energy base effects take 2+ quarters to flush. The crisis transmits to US assets via the sticky CPI/rates channel, so a Hormuz deal helps oil-IMPORTERS (India) far more than US assets. A SUSTAINED oil-down (if the deal holds) would, over quarters, ease the energy-CPI impulse -- but not on a headline.

MAY CPI 4.2% YoY (0.5% m/m) -- 3-YEAR HIGH, highest since Apr 2023. Energy +23.5% YoY = ~60% of the monthly increase (the oil->CPI->Fed chain is now the PRIMARY crisis-transmission channel into US assets); core 2.9% with its own momentum. Fed-cut hopes dead; ~98% hold priced for 17 Jun, but the market now prices a December HIKE as live base-risk (odds disputed across sources: CME FedWatch, Yahoo ~30% rising, Polymarket >50%). This print does NOT un-print on Gulf de-escalation -- energy base effects take 2+ quarters to flush.

**THE DECEMBER FED-HIKE ODDS JUMPED TO ~70% (FROM ~45% A WEEK AGO) — THE ENGINE UNDER THE METALS COLLAPSE, THE FIRM DOLLAR, AND THE 4.52% 10Y, AND THE REASON THE WAR-RISK COMPLEX DID NOT BID.** Oil-fed inflation + a hot May jobs print pushed market-implied December Fed-HIKE odds to ~70%, lifting DXY (~99.9) and pinning the 10Y at 4.52% — which crushed the precious-metals complex (gold -2.1% to a ~23-March low, silver -24.7% 1M) and is the proximate reason zero-yield gold could not bid a three-country missile strike on US installations (an INFLATION/real-yields event, not risk-off). CRITICAL FOR DEPLOYMENT TIMING: this repricing is TRIGGERED by the crisis (oil -> inflation) but would NOT fully reverse on Gulf de-escalation — sticky inflation + a hot labour market persist INDEPENDENTLY of the war; the war is paradoxically gold-negative / dollar-positive via the Fed channel. TAG DISCIPLINE: a hike is PRICED/odds (~70%, now data-backed by the May jobs print), NOT EXECUTED; the 2Y at 3.63% sits below the funds-range top — encode 'hike odds ~70% / higher-for-longer hardened,' not 'hike confirmed.' RBI mirrored: HELD repo 5.25% (3rd straight hold, neutral, hike-bias). 'Fed signals rate HIKE' trigger stays watching (the ~70% odds advance toward it but a market repricing does not fire it).
sources: Market-implied December Fed-HIKE odds ~70% (from ~45% a week ago), on hot May jobs + oil-fed inflation (CME FedWatch / market-implied)
Market data 2026-06-09/10 (10Y 4.52%, 2Y 3.63%, DXY 99.91; gold -2.1% to ~23-March low, silver -24.7% 1M)
Intel 2026-06-10 (RBI held repo 5.25%, neutral, hike-bias); war-risk complex (gold, Brent, VIX) did not bid the maximum kinetic escalation
recent_signals.verification: December Fed-HIKE odds ~70% from ~45% = REPORTED (market-implied / CME FedWatch), now data-backed by the confirmed May jobs print; 10Y 4.52% / 2Y 3.63% / DXY 99.91 = CONFIRMED market data; 'engine under the metals collapse + non-bid' = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS anchored to the confirmed gold/silver tape + firm DXY; 'triggered by crisis but does not reverse on de-escalation' = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (sticky inflation + hot labour market are domestic). TAG DISCIPLINE: hike priced/odds, NOT executed (2Y below funds-range top). RBI hold 5.25% = CONFIRMED. No trigger to active ('Fed signals rate HIKE' requires an actual Fed signal, not a market repricing).

recent_signals.headline: **HOT MAY JOBS KILLED FED-CUT HOPES AND PRICED A QUARTER-POINT HIKE BY YEAR-END (CME FEDWATCH) — DATA-BACKED THIS TIME, AND THE RATES LEG OF A BROAD RISK-OFF.** May NFP +172K vs ~85K expected, U-rate 4.3%, AHE +0.3% m/m / +3.4% YoY, Mar/Apr revised +93K — a decisively hot read. Unlike the 3/4-June 'hike' framing (downgraded because the 2Y did not corroborate it), this is a genuine BLS datum that materially repriced the path: CME FedWatch moved to a quarter-point HIKE by year-end, 10Y 4.54% (highest since 21 May), DXY +0.7% to 100.07. A DOMESTIC MACRO datum — does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. Transmission: hot jobs -> no cuts -> 10Y 4.54% -> duration-tech multiple compression, the rates co-driver (with Broadcom's AI miss) of the 5-June rout (S&P -2.6%, NASDAQ -4.2%, SOX largest 1D plunge since March 2020). THE TELL: gold FELL 3.1% and oil FELL 2% as 10Y/DXY rose and VIX spiked ~40% — a rates/AI de-risking, not a war risk-off. RBI mirrored: HELD repo 5.25% (3rd straight hold, neutral, hike-bias).
sources: BLS May jobs report (+172K vs ~85K exp, U-rate 4.3%, AHE +0.3% m/m / +3.4% YoY, Mar/Apr revised +93K)
Markets dossier 2026-06-06 (CME FedWatch quarter-point hike by year-end; 10Y 4.54%, DXY 100.07 +0.7%; rates -> duration-tech channel into AI rout)
Intel 2026-06-06 (RBI held repo 5.25%, neutral, hike-bias); cross-asset tell gold -3.1% / oil -2% as VIX +~40%
recent_signals.verification: May jobs +172K / U-rate 4.3% / AHE +3.4% YoY / +93K revisions = CONFIRMED (BLS print); 10Y 4.54% / DXY 100.07 = CONFIRMED (market data); quarter-point-hike-by-year-end = REPORTED (CME FedWatch market-implied) and now DATA-BACKED by the BLS print (distinct from the 3/4-June framing that the fact-check downgraded), though the 2Y at 3.62% still sits below the funds-range top — encode as 'hike priced/odds, higher-for-longer hardened,' not 'hike confirmed'; RBI hold 5.25% = CONFIRMED; rates/AI-de-risking-not-war attribution = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS anchored to confirmed gold -3.1% + oil -2% as 10Y/DXY rose. No trigger to active (the 'Fed signals rate HIKE' trigger requires an actual Fed signal/action, not a market-implied repricing on a data print).

recent_signals.headline: **THE RATE-PATH REGIME HELD — METALS STABILISED AS OIL EASED; 2Y HELD 3.62%. TAG DISCIPLINE ON THE 'HIKE' FRAMING.** The 4-June market dossier cites 'Fed expected to HIKE >=25bps by year-end (CME FedWatch)' — the SAME framing the 3-June fact-check downgraded; the 2Y holding flat at 3.62% (below the funds-range top) is consistent with cuts-priced-out / higher-for-longer, NOT a priced hike. Encoded as higher-for-longer, NOT 'hike confirmed.' The read confirmed in mirror image: Brent down ~1% -> less inflation impulse -> metals STOPPED falling (gold a non-mover at -0.2%), confirming gold trades the real-yield/Fed channel not the crisis. May NFP (Fri 5 Jun) expected light ~85K. India's RBI mirrored: held 5.25% (neutral, hike-bias). The PPI/Warsh regime is structural, not reversible on a Hormuz deal.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-06-04 C6/D3 ('Fed expected to hike >=25bps by year-end, CME FedWatch'; rates backdrop suppressing gold); fed node 3-June fact-check (2Y 3.62%, CME ~65% hold)
recent_signals.verification: Metals-stabilised-as-oil-eased = CONFIRMED (market data). The 'Fed hike by year-end' framing = REPORTED (markets dossier citing CME FedWatch) but NOT elevated to CONFIRMED — it is the same claim the 3-June fact-check downgraded, and the 2Y at 3.62% does not corroborate a priced hike. Encoded as higher-for-longer/cuts-pushed-out. RBI hold 5.25% = CONFIRMED. No trigger to active.

recent_signals.headline: **SOFTER APRIL MONTHLY PCE (+0.4% vs +0.5% EXP) DROVE THE GOLD REBOUND AND EQUITY RECORDS — BUT CUTS STILL PRICED OUT.** April PCE headline 3.8% YoY (3-yr high), core 3.3% in-line, monthly +0.4% below the +0.5% expected. The softer monthly read dialed back rate-HIKE bets (lifting gold) and lifted equities; Fed cuts remain fully priced out (PPI/Warsh inflation regime, structural through Q3). Does not reverse on a Hormuz deal.
sources: TheStreet 28 May (S&P/Nasdaq records despite highest PCE in ~3yr); markets dossier D1, C4
recent_signals.verification: April PCE 3.8% YoY headline / 3.3% core / +0.4% monthly (vs +0.5% exp) = CONFIRMED (data); cuts priced out = market-implied; structural-through-Q3 regime = analyst assessment. No weight elevation beyond the data linkage.

recent_signals.headline: **A4 APRIL PCE DEFLATOR RELEASED TODAY (MAY 28) — HEADLINE PCE 3.8% (HIGHEST SINCE MAY 2023); CORE PCE 3.3% (AS EXPECTED, STRONGEST SINCE OCT). FED CUTS NOW FULLY PRICED OUT; MARKET BEGINNING TO PRICE HIKES.** (1) **The print and its caveat**: the Fed's preferred inflation gauge confirmed the stagflation regime — headline 3.8% is a fresh ~3-year high; core 3.3% printed IN-LINE with expectations, so the rates reaction was MUTED (10Y -0.7% 1D to 4.45%) rather than a fresh spike. The signal is not the daily move; it is that the easing path is closed indefinitely and the asymmetry has bent toward a HIKE — cuts are fully priced out. (2) **The transmission to gold (the day's cleanest channel)**: oil↑→inflation↑→real-yields↑ kept real yields elevated, which pinned GOLD to a 2-month low BELOW $4,400 even on a day with a live US-Iran ballistic-missile exchange (IRGC fired one calibrated missile at a US base in Kuwait, intercepted, zero casualty). Zero-yield gold lost the opportunity-cost contest decisively — the crisis bid the dollar, not gold. (3) **Institutional frame**: Fed held at 3.5-3.75% for a 3rd consecutive meeting under new Chair Kevin Warsh on a historic-dissent vote (first multi-dissent of this magnitude since 1992) — operative context, with the April PCE print as today's NEW input. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: the energy contribution reverses on an oil retrace; core is sticky and structural through Q3 2026.
sources: BEA/BLS PCE release April 2026 (primary, May 28 — headline 3.8% highest since May 2023; core 3.3% as expected, strongest since Oct)
Markets dossier 2026-05-28 A4 / Section A (10Y -0.7% 1D to 4.45% — muted on in-line core; cuts fully priced out, hikes being priced)
Markets dossier 2026-05-28 (oil→inflation→real-yield channel pinned gold below $4,400 to a 2-month low on a missile-exchange day)
recent_signals.verification: April PCE headline 3.8% / core 3.3% = CONFIRMED (BEA primary release May 28); core in-line with expectations = CONFIRMED; cuts-fully-priced-out / hikes-being-priced = CONFIRMED market pricing; real-yield channel pinning gold below $4,400 = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS supported by confirmed gold-tape; 3rd-meeting hold under Warsh / 1992-dissent frame = CONFIRMED (carry-forward operative context).

recent_signals.headline: A2 APRIL CPI 3.8% YoY (BLS MAY 12) = THREE-YEAR HIGH (highest since May 2023); +0.6% MoM matching expectations; CORE +2.8% YoY / +0.4% MoM (highest monthly print since Jan 2025); ENERGY contributed >40% of headline gain (+3.8% MoM, +17.9% YoY); GASOLINE +28.4% YoY; SHELTER +0.6%. BLS itself flagged inflation 'a problem beyond Iran war impacts.' First cycle inflation print to fully reflect oil pass-through. US 10Y bid at 4.46% (+5bps); real-yield channel tightens; gold +0.8% only on 3-year-high CPI (real-yield channel competing with safe-haven bid); URA -5.0% (positioning unwind). S&P 500 -0.29% May 12 to 7,392 (off May 11 record). Fed cannot cut while Brent >$100 — 90%-confidence trigger ('Brent sustained >$100 for 4+ weeks') activated April 22 now fully visible in macro data. Fed-rate-cut-driven US equity rally thesis OUT for 2026 unless either Brent breaks $95 sustained OR core inflation reverses <2.5% — neither imminent. Fed-speak embargo through May 15.
sources: CNN Business 'US inflation rose to 3.8% in April'
recent_signals.verification: CPI 3.8% YoY = CONFIRMED (BLS primary statistic, multi-source); core +2.8% / +0.4% MoM = CONFIRMED (BLS primary); energy contribution >40% of headline gain = CONFIRMED (BLS data); US 10Y +5bps = CONFIRMED tape (yfinance).

historical_summaries.summary: 4 signals. FOMC voted 11-1 to hold rates at 3 | Market removed basically every rate cut for 2026, now pricing odds of a HIKE | Fed remains trapped -- 10Y at 4
19 signals. Eurozone inflation surged to 2 | Fed trapped in stagflation -- ISM Prices Index at 78 | DXY fell to 99
